subject: Eutropia, a robed humanoid figure with a tall pointed headdress and red facial markings, holds a small bowl in each outstretched hand while surrounded by swirling blue-green magical energy
description: A humanoid figure stands centered in a wide open space, arms extended outward at sides with each hand cradling a small rounded bowl or vessel. The figure wears layered white robes wrapped tightly at the torso, with decorative brown leather or cloth wrappings at the forearms and waist. A tall pointed white headdress rises above the head, and a long white veil or cloth drapes to shoulder level. Red markings are visible on the forehead and there are red or crimson accent elements in the robes. The figure's eyes are closed or lowered in a meditative expression of calm focus. Surrounding the figure, swirling arcs of blue and teal magical energy fill the frame — suggesting wind or ocean current — along with faint golden-green accents. To the left a large white slab or tablet leans partially visible; to the right a glowing green-blue pillar or monument stands in the distance. The flavor text confirms this is Eutropia, twice-favored by Nylea (god of the hunt/nature, associated with winds and green) and Thassa (god of the sea, associated with blue). The bowls she holds likely represent offerings to both deities.
foreground: Robed figure with tall headdress, red facial markings, holding a bowl in each outstretched hand
background: Swirling blue and teal magical energy arcs, stone monuments, green-blue atmospheric glow
emotion: meditative calm; eyes downcast or closed, arms open in an act of devotion or offering
